lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Have you had any luck yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid not Ceril.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: That's SIR Ceril you blooming scoundrel! Show respect when addressing someone of my rank! It's strange you haven't recovered it yet though as Jones seemed quite sure of their location.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, well, just unlucky I guess.

sections.title: Using any item on Claus' range

lines.speaker: Claus Carnillean
lines.text: Oi - I don't want people messing around with my range!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. What is this building?

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: This is the home of Sir Ceril Carnillean of the noble Carnillean family. You're welcome to look around, but I'm afraid I'll have to keep an eye on you.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Recently we've been having a real problem with thieves and strange cultists coming out of the forest for some reason.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a shame.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Yes, well, these things are bound to happen when you're as wealthy as the Carnilleans.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you any more info on the Carnilleans?

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: There's a lot I could tell you about the Carnillean family history. However, I'm afraid if I did speak about such matters I would be at risk of losing my job.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How long have you worked here?

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Long enough to know the Carnilleans are not as innocent or noble as they seem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok then, take care.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: You too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Blooming, thieving, weirdo cultists! Why don't they leave me alone? String 'em all up, that's what I say!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: It's those blooming cultists from the forest! Those freaks keep breaking into my house!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have they taken much?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: They first broke in months ago and stole a suit of armour. The strange thing is that they've broken in four times since but taken nothing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you are...?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Why, I am Sir Ceril Carnillean! We really are quite a famous bloodline... who've played a vital role in the politics of Ardougne for many generations.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Perhaps you would be able to assist me in returning the stolen armour? For a modest cash reward of course!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks. I've got other plans.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: No wonder I'm the one with the big house, and you're the one on the streets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, of course, I'd be happy to help.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: That's very noble of you sirrah! I caught a glimpse of the thieves leaving but due to... uh.. yeah, my cold I was unable to give chase.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: They're some kind of crazy cult who dress all in black, and hang out near the cave entrance in the forest south of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you know that?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: My old butler, Higson, once followed them to their hideout there. Unfortunately the next night he died in his sleep.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's awful!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: No, it's ok. A replacement arrived the next day. He's been great - cooks an excellent broth!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok. I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You probably deserve it.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: And who are YOU to judge ME? Hmmm. You look like a peasant anyway. I'm wasting my time talking to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem uptight. I'll leave you alone.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Yes, I doubt you could help anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: So. You've returned. Now do you want to know the truth about the 'honourable' Carnilleans?

sections.title: Using the raft without the correct water valve settings
Turning the sewer valve to the right
Turning the sewer valve to the left
Sucessfully using the raft with the correct water valve settings

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: How did YOU get in here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've come for the Carnillean family armour. Hand it over, or face the consequences.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: I thought I made it clear to the butler you could not be allowed to interfere with our mission. The incompetent fool must be going soft.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the butler's part of your sordid little cult huh? Why is it ALWAYS the butler? I should have known...

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Well you won't live long enough to tell anyone! DIE!!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Hazeel Cultist
lines.text: What? Who are you and why did you come here? It doesn't matter, leave now adventurer. While you still can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Ceril.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: That's SIR Ceril to you, you impudent scamp. Show a bit of respect to your betters. And shouldn't you be recovering my armour?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah yeah, I'm on it, I'm ON it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know the Carnilleans?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: You mind your business, I'll mind mine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I KNOW you're hiding something. I've heard theres a cult hideout down here.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: If you want to stay healthy you'll leave now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have my orders.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: So... that two faced cold hearted snob has made you fall for his propaganda eh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sir Ceril Carnillean is a man of honour!

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Is he now? Is he REALLY? There is a lot more to the Carnilleans than meets the eye you fool...

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: And none of it is honourable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: The Carnillean home does not belong to them. The builder of the house was Lord Hazeel, one of the Mahjarrat followers of Zamorak. Many years ago there was a civil war

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: in this land, and the hateful Saradominists declared war upon all Zamorakians who lived here. Lord Hazeel nobly would not repent his beliefs, and the Carnilleans harrassed

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Hazeel and his followers for many decades. One fateful night, under cover of darkness, they stormed his home in an angry mob torturing and butchering all loyal

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Zamorakians they encountered inside. The following morning, the Carnillean forefathers moved into the empty household and claimed it as their own.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: They have grown fat on the hard work of Lord Hazeel ever since. Unluckily for them Lord Hazeel as a Mahjarrat had access to powers and enchantments they knew nothing

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: about, and made preparations for his return when first they began to storm his home. Soon the day will come when he will return to wreak his vengeance upon the thieves!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The politics and histories of Ardougne do not concern me. I have been given a job and intend to see it through to the end.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Well then friend, perhaps I can offer you a different job then? Sooner or later Our master WILL return to this land; those faithful to him will be well rewarded.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Join our cult and assist us in his restoration, and you will be well rewarded; try and prevent his return and suffer the wrath of Zamorak and the Mahjarrat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're crazy, I'd never help you.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Then you are a fool. Go back to your small minded mundane little life; you will never know the glories you could have tasted as one of us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what would I have to do?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: You must prove your loyalty to our cause. Killing one of the Carnillean household should be sufficient proof of your dedication to resurrecting our master Lord Hazeel.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: So what say you adventurer? Join our side against the Carnillean thieves?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. I won't do it.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Then you are a fool. Go back to your small minded, mundane little life; you will never know the glories you could have tasted as one of us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, count me in.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Excellent. It takes a rare character to see through the cursed Carnillean lies. I can tell you are a [man/lady] of exactly the right character to join the followers of Hazeel.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Here. Take this poison. Pour it into their food, and once the deed is done, return here and speak to me once more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've heard enough of your rubbish!

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Then leave, fool!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Have you had any luck yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Ceril. Yes, I found their hideout.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: SIR Ceril to you. But good work there! BLOOMING good work! Hand over the armour then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... yeah... about that... I interrogated a cult member at the hideout's entrance, but he managed to escape into the sewer system.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seems they have some sort of grievance against you and your family. Something about some guy called Hazeel?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Hazeel? Uh... umm... err.. hmmmm.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Nope, they're obviously a bunch of blooming loonies! Go find them and bring me back my blooming armour! Nobody steals from Sir Ceril Carnillean!!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello madam.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: I hope you found those awful hooligan cultists! I simply cannot sleep at night knowing they're loose!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know, I know, I'm working on it ok?

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: I don't know, honestly I don't. There are some very odd folk around these parts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Hazeel Cultist
lines.text: What? How did you get in here? Who are you and why do you come here? It doesn't matter, you will not live long enough to tell anyone of our hideout!

sections.title: Talking to Alomone before obtaining the armour

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: How did YOU get in here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've come for the Carnillean family armour. Hand it over, or face the consequences.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: I thought I made it clear to the butler you could not be allowed to interfere with our mission. The incompetent fool must be going soft.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the butler's part of your sordid little cult huh? Why is it ALWAYS the butler? I should have known...

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Well you won't live long enough to tell anyone! DIE!!!

sections.title: Talking to Alomone after obtaining the armour

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Alomone.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Out of my way idiot! We are preparing matters your puny mind could not hope to understand!

sections.title: Talking to Ceril before obtaining the armour or after losing it[confirmation needed]

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ceril. How are you?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: I'm fine. Where's my armour?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... I didn't get it yet... I DID find the cult's hideout though!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Well what are you waiting for? I'm not paying you to blooming sight see you know!

sections.title: Talking to a Hazeeel cultist after obtaining the armour

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Hazeel Cultist
lines.text: What? An intruder? HERE? How did you get in here? You must leave! NOW!

sections.title: Talking to Clivet after obtaining the armour

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: You again! You meddle in our affairs too much! When Lord Hazeel returns to this realm he will lay a mighty vengeance upon you!

sections.title: Talking to Henryeta after obtaining the armour

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: Oh, hello again adventurer. I trust you did not have too much trouble dealing with those hooligans?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nah, it was a piece of cake.

sections.title: Talking to Butler Jones with the armour

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Jones. We need to talk.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Yes? You need some help with your quest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Drop the act Jones. I know you're in league with the cultists.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: W-what? N-no I'm not, don't be silly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Listen pal, I went to your hideout, I heard them talking about you. You're fooling nobody.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: You think you're so smart don't you? You might think you know what's going on but you have no idea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know all I need to know and when I reveal the truth you're going directly to jail.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Oh, you think so do you? You think that silly old fool Ceril will take YOUR word over MINE? He trusts me completely!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll see about that.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: This is your last warning adventurer: keep your nose out of our business.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look! I've recovered your armour!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Well done! I must say I am very impressed! Come on, hand it over.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Before we send you on your way with your payment, I'll just get Jones to whip you up a batch of his special broth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd rather not if it's all the same to you. I overheard the cultists talking, and apparently Jones is in league with them.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: W-what? RIGHT! You! Come with me upstairs! We're going to blooming well sort this out right now once and for all!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: W-what? RIGHT! You! We're going to blooming well sort this out right now once and for all!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Jones! This commoner says you had something to do with the theft of my armour. What do you have to say for yourself about that?

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: It wasn't me m'lud. I am, as you know, a loyal servant.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Humph. Quite right too. I cannot fathom why this scoundrel would accuse you of such a crime without evidence to back up his accusations.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Right. I have decided. I have given my word as a nobleman to reward you for your efforts in retrieving my armour.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: but I must also compensate Jones for this terrible slander you have made against him.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Now take it and leave you scoundrel! And don't darken my doorstep again!

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Don't worry m'lud, this fool won't be bothering us any more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hello again adventurer. Still hanging around here eh? I'll be honest, I'm glad someone of your ability is around to assist me in case of trouble.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You owe me money.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: I owe you nothing you scoundrel! Now get out or I'll have Jones throw you out!

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Cretin. Did you think you could 'save the day' just by accusing me? We've been planning this for YEARS. your meddling has been nothing more than a minor inconvenience.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: When Lord Hazeel returns, the likes of you and the arrogant Carnilleans will be first to suffer his vengeance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not if I can help it!

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: That's basically my point. You cannot help it. Now begone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Henryeta.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: You! Don't think you can go accusing our trusted staff and then waltz up to me as though nothing has happened!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Everything I said was the truth.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: HA! Don't be so ridiculous! Next you'll be trying to tell me Jones murdered our old butler!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: I have no time to waste with the likes of you do-gooder.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: You again. Mark my words meddler; when Lord Hazeel returns you will be punished most severely for your interference in our schemes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Claus the chef
lines.text: Hello! How are you today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not bad, thanks.

lines.speaker: Claus the chef
lines.text: Good good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello guard.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hello adventurer. I hear you accused Jones the butler of being an accomplice of that loony cult. Is that true?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's true, but nobody believes me.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You have my utter faith adventurer. I can't openly defy my employer, Sir Ceril, but I'd like you to know that I think you're on the right track there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, thanks! That actually does make me feel a bit better!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You've already proven to me that you're a law-abiding citizen. Besides, I always found it suspicious that Jones would just show up here on the doorstep the very day after the old butler died in such suspicious

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: circumstances. Good luck in clearing your name. You have my support.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ceril!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: What do you want now you scoundrel?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look what I've found in the cupboard!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: I... I don't believe it! Poison! JONES!

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: You called m'lud?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: JONES! Just WHAT is this POISON doing here???

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: P-p-poison??? yeah... uh... well, hmm... Oh, it's for Killing rats.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Yes, that's right. Killing rats. I am but a loyal butler sir.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Rats eh? Yes, fair enough. Sorry to have doubted you old boy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then how about this?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Wh-what??? I've seen this amulet before... The thieves that broke in... One of them was wearing an identical amulet! Jones! I don't believe it!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Jones! We trusted you! We took you into our home!

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: You senile old fool... It was all too easy! I should have killed you and your pathetic family weeks ago!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: To think we took you in and trusted you... and this is how you repay us...

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: GUARD!

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Take him away. Attempted murder and burglary.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Don't think this is the last you have heard of me Ceril. You and your family will pay dearly for this. GLORY TO HAZEEL! ALL PRAISE ZAMORAK!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Now now. Come along quietly. There'll be no revenge where YOU'RE going - Port Sarim Jail.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: It looks like I am indebted to you sirrah!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem. We all make mistakes.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: But if it weren't for you... My whole family... we could have been... I apologise for my harshness before.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: the very least I can do is to reward you for your noble efforts, and to offer my sincerest apologies as a Lord and gentleman.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you Lord Ceril.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: No, no, thank YOU! Feel free to stop by anytime adventurer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Oh no... not you again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: WHERE is the cult hideout?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: You're more of a fool than you look if you think you will ever find it. When Lord Hazeel is revived you will be first to grovel for his mercy!

sections.title: Talking to Clivet after accepting his job

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: You have a mission for us adventurer. Go to the Carnilliean household and poison their meal to prove your loyalty.

sections.title: Losing the poison and talking to Clivet

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: You have a mission for us adventurer. Go to the Carnilliean household and poison their meal to prove your loyalty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost the poison you gave me...

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Useless. Take this and don't mess up this time. Lord Hazeel is depending on you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Hazeel Cultist
lines.text: Leave me alone, can't you see I'm busy? We have a lot to do preparing for the imminent return of Lord Hazeel

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Can't you see I'm busy???

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hello. I hear tell you're after that loony cult who broke in the other night. It always gladdens me when civilians assist the law like this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How long have you worked here?

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Long enough to know the Carnilleans are not as innocent or noble as they seem.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Have you had any luck yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid not Ceril.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: That's SIR Ceril you blooming scoundrel! Show respect when addressing someone of my rank! It's strange you haven't recovered it yet though as Jones seemed quite sure of their location.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, well, just unlucky I guess.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello madam.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: I hope you found those awful hooligan cultists! I simply cannot sleep at night knowing they're loose!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid not.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: Humph. You really are useless aren't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yup. Guess I am.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: More toys! I want MORE TOYS!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I don't have any.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: Sweets! Gimme sweets!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope, sorry, I don't have sweets either.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: I hate you! HATE YOU! Where's my mommy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hiya.

lines.speaker: Claus the chef
lines.text: You're that adventurer the family asked to help get those weird cultists who keep breaking in, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's me.

lines.speaker: Claus the chef
lines.text: Good luck with that!

sections.title: Using the poison on the range

lines.speaker: Claus the chef
lines.text: Hello there. Caught any of those weird thieves yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope. Afraid not.

lines.speaker: Claus the chef
lines.text: Well, keep at it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: No, no I am very far from OK. Those hooligans slaughtered my precious Scruffy! I shall never recover! I am emotionally scarred for life!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It WAS only a dog you know...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Hello friend. I heard about your handiwork. Quite amusing really. I'm sure Hazeel will be pleased anyway; Keep up the good work!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello youngster.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: Someone killed scruffy. I liked scruffy. He never told me off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah. It's a real shame.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: I want my mommy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Oh.. the inhumanity... the cruelty.. the misery... the pain... my son is a good boy, really, but how could he give his dinner to Scruffy without having the servants test it for poison first? How? How could he be so

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: thoughtless and careless? He knows we are all under threat!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Scruffy?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: He's been with our family for twenty years... that's 140 in dog years! The poor dog... what did HE ever do to deserve such a fate???

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your DOG got poisoned???

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not right.

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: I agree! I hope whichever evildoer is responsible gets the full weight of the law brought upon them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh.... yeah... me too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hello. The sarge is furious with me! Apparently those loony cultists have been back somehow without me noticing! I got a right roasting earlier...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I don't exactly know what they got up to in the house... but that's besides the point. Sir Ceril was extremely upset, and I don't blame him.

sections.title: Talking to a Hazeel cultist again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hiya.

lines.speaker: Hazeel Cultist
lines.text: Hello. Oh, are you new?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Hazeel Cultist
lines.text: Well, it's always good to have a new recruit. Soon we shall retrive Hazeels' scroll and the ritual to revive him can begin!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I poured the poison into the Carnillean's meal as requested. It didn't quite go to plan however.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Yes, we heard all about it from one of our sources. Hazeel has eyes everywhere! You have now proved your loyalty and will be rewarded when Hazeel is revived!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok. So what's next?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Here. Wear this amulet; it is called The Sign Of Hazeel. Not only will it identify you as one of our brotherhood to other followers but it is also key to finding our hideout.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: But of course, you already knew that, as you already have The Sign Of Hazeel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How does this amulet help do that then?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Hazeel in his wisdom when he built this lair did so by making it inaccessible unless the flow of the sewers is controlled exactly by the sewer valves above ground.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Each sewer valve must be correctly set before the water level is such that you may enter. The secret of the correct settings is contained in these amulets which honour him.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Starting from left to right, follow the design of the amulet to locate each sewer valve, and then turn each so as to follow the amulet design.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: When you solve the sequence you may enter our hideout where our leader, Alomone, awaits you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Well well well... So we have a new recruit. Clivet told me of your desire to join us in our glorious task to resurrect the mighty Hazeel from his slumber.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: To accomplish this, we require the ancient words of summoning which will restore his shattered body, so that he can bring vengeance upon his enemies.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Hazeel in his mighty cunning anticipated that he might be defeated and secured within his home somewhere a powerful magical scroll that could restore him.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: The words to this powerful enchantment are hidden within the Carnillean mansion, right under the nose of his unsuspecting foes. We already have an agent in place there

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: in their Butler Jones, a faithful follower to Hazeel. His loyalty to Hazeel is beyond questioning, yet he has been unsuccessful in locating the scroll containing the spell so far.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Go back to the mansion, and assist us in finding this enchantment, so that together we can restore Hazeel to his true power and glory!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Hello once more adventurer. All we need is Hazeels' magical scroll to bring him forth once more to this world.

sections.title: Talking to a Hazeel Cultist again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Hazeel Cultist
lines.text: Well hello! Until we have retrieved the scroll of Hazeel we cannot resurrect our master. You must find it and bring it to us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Alomone.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Hazeel has waited long enough to return! It is IMPERATIVE you find that spell!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah yeah yeah, I'm on it, I'm ON it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hello again adventurer. Still hanging around here eh? Let me know if you spot any of those loony cultists sniffing around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ceril. How are you?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Oh cruel world! Scruffy... I knew you well... I... just... don't think I can... go on without...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Loosen up already. It was only a DOG.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: Mommy said you're here to kill all the nasty men that keep breaking in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something like that.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: Can I watch?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: I'm sorry. I am far too depressed to talk to anybody right now. Oh... poor Scruffy...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah. Poor Scruffy.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Hello again friend. I see you have the mark now; You should keep it covered up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That? Oh, it's nothing. Just an old amulet.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Hello again friend. I see you decided to keep the mark of Hazeel discreetly about you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mark of Hazeel? I'm sure I don't know what you mean.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: You don't have to pretend with me, friend! Our cause is one and the same; the sooner Lord Hazeel is avenged the better for this city... and for us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So do you have any idea where the scroll with the enchantment is?

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: No idea I'm afraid. I KNOW it's somewhere in this house, but for the life of me I can't find it anywhere. I've searched high and low for it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And Sir Ceril doesn't suspect a thing?

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Ha! That silly old fool? He can't see the forest for the trees.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll keep on looking then...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Claus.

lines.speaker: Claus the chef
lines.text: I... just CAN'T understand it! How? HOW could someone poison my delicious food without me even noticing??? I... I just don't understand!

lines.speaker: Claus the chef
lines.text: Oh man... I hope I don't lose my job over this... but HOW? How did they do it? And why would the villain want to kill poor Scruffy anyway? He was such a good dog...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah. It's a real mystery, huh?

sections.title: Searching the crate in the kitchen
Using the Knock-at wall with the key
Talking to Jones after obtaining the scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Jones.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Have you recovered the scroll?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have it right here.

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Incredible!

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Quick, get it back to our hideout! These are glorious times indeed adventurer, when the mighty Hazeel returns things are REALLY going to change around here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... yeah... about that.. I don't exactly have it right now...

lines.speaker: Butler Jones
lines.text: Incompetent fool! The future of our entire plans relies on that scroll! You MUST find it and take it back to our hideout as soon as possible!

sections.title: Talking to Ceril after obtaining the scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Ceril. How's things?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: SIR Ceril! SIR! And I'm not sure but I think the thieves have been back in my house.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What makes you say that?

lines.speaker: Ceril Carnillean
lines.text: Well, either I'm going crazy, or theres been an intruder in Jones' room upstairs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, heh, ok then.

sections.title: Talking to Philipe after obtaining the scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello youngster.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: Why are you still here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just looking around.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: Have you got me some toys?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: Then I don't like you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gee. I guess I'll just have to live with that then.

sections.title: Talking to Claus after obtaining the scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Claus the chef
lines.text: Those animals... how could they do that to poor Scruffy? What kind of lowlife would do that???

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Get over it, it was only a dog.

sections.title: Talking to the guard after obtaining the scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello guard.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hello adventurer. I hope you find the cult soon we have a horrible suspicion that there's been another burglary.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's worrying.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes, it is. What's worse is I have no idea how they keep doing it. It's like they do it under my very nose!

sections.title: Talking to Clivet after obtaining the scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Have you acquired the enchanted scroll required to resurrect Hazeel yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, it was in the house all along. Jones can't have looked that hard for it.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: At last! The pieces are all in place for the glorious resurrection of Lord Hazeel! Now is the starting point for a new page in history, and our chance for vengeance!

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Quickly now! Take the scroll to Alomone so that we may begin the ritual... and when Lord Hazeel returns I promise you that you shall be well rewarded!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... yeah... about that... I seem to have, errm, misplayed it somewhere...

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Incompetent cretin! Return to the Carnillean household and do not return again without it!

sections.title: Talking to a Hazeel cultist after obtaining the scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Hazeel Cultist
lines.text: Welcome back adventurer! Since you have brought us the scroll of Hazeel we can now perform the resurrection ceremony! Soon our master will return!

sections.title: Talking to Alomone after obtaining the scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Have you brought me the scroll of restoration to complete the ritual?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yep. Got it right here.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: FINALLY! Quickly, give it to me and I will begin the ritual!

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Yes... YES! With the words contained within this scroll our Lord shall return to this realm and bring destruction to our enemies!

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Watch adventurer, and witness the glorious rebirth of Hazeel!

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Lord Hazeel... Lord Zamorak... I call upon the powers of evil... The forces of chaos... The strength of hatred...

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Sentente sillaberi junque dithmento! Ia! Ia! dextrimon encanto! termando... imcando... solly enty rando... sentente! Ia! Ia! Indenti zaggarati g'thxa!

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Dintenta! Sententa! Retenta! Q'exjta! Ia! Sottottott! Ia! Dysmenta junque fammatio svelken! Sottey! Sentey! SOLOMENT!

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: My loyal followers. I have pride in you all. Never did I expect to return to this land and I see I have much to attend to.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Soon this world will cower once more at the name Hazeel, and my fury will blaze across mine enemies just as my loyal followers gain their rewards.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Adventurer. I know that thy efforts were principal in my return to this plane of existence. I offer you my thanks directly.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: You may not be a follower of Zamorak directly but your cunning and treachery mark you as a friend to the Mahjarrat. Rest assured; I will call on your assistance again.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Weak as I am now, my strength shall return, and my followers will be greatly rewarded when my powers peak once more. Join me in my cause as my servant, and all

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: the riches of this plane shall be yours to share, in domination over all who oppose my kind. You would be wise to pledge yourself now while you still may.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I serve nobody but myself.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Your insolence shall be overlooked this time for I am in truth in your debt. Come close: I shall reward you with wealth such as you deserve.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Although your true reward will be later, when Zamorak and I, together, lay waste to this miserable planet, and you are spared as an ally to us.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: And now I must leave you loyal subjects for time is short, and I have many pressing matters to address before I may make my return. I must leave now, and head Northwards

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: to join my fellow mahjarrat and regain my strength and powers. I shall return when I am ready to fight my enemies and much blood shall flow across these lands!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope, sorry.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: You must find it and bring it to me! Without it we cannot resurrect Hazeel and our efforts to do so will fail!
